contract_discrepancies.contract: derive SPECHUMD[0] from 2 m dewpoint and PSFC
contract_discrepancies.implementation: default policy follows oracle; optional 'dewpoint' policy is implemented and tested
contract_discrepancies.oracle: met_em SPECHUMD[0] is -1 everywhere; METGRID.TBL fills const(-1)

contract_discrepancies.contract_schema_comment: schema constant lists (10, 40) while noting met_em SOIL_LAYERS=[40,10]
contract_discrepancies.oracle: met_em SOIL_LAYERS values are [40, 10] and ST/SM stack is [010040, 000010]

objective: v0.3.0 S1 native AIFS GRIB2 forcing decode/normalize

oracle_findings.global_range_sanity: The raw AIFS source is a global grid. The sampled GRIB and matching ungrib intermediate contain PSFC below 50000 Pa and ST below 220 K at global points, so those contract sanity ranges are recorded but not used as the oracle-fidelity verdict.
oracle_findings.soil_stack_order: Checked met_em oracle stores ST/SM layer 0 as 010040 and layer 1 as 000010, with SOIL_LAYERS [40, 10].
oracle_findings.surface_spechumd: Checked met_em oracle has SPECHUMD[0] == -1 everywhere for the sampled case; this follows METGRID.TBL.ARW fill_lev=200100:const(-1).
